title: Migration to Uniface 10.4
text: Migration to Uniface 10.4 How to migrate to Uniface 10.4 When you install Uniface 10.4 , it creates a new Repository. It is not possible to run the Uniface 10 IDE (or later) against an older Repository, such as Uniface 10.3 or Uniface 9. You must therefore migrate your existing sources to the new Repository. Whether you are migrating from Uniface 9, the Uniface 10.3 Enterprise Edition, or the Uniface Community Edition, the basic migration steps are the same: Export your application definitions from your existing Uniface installation. Import the definitions into the new Repository. Uniface detects that the definitions must be migrated and starts the Migration Utility which converts the existing definitions to match the new Repository requirements. If you have customized your development and test environment, you will need to configure Uniface 10.4 so that it can access the needed database and file resources, and servers. Compile and test the migrated application. Depending on the version you are migrating from and the size and complexity of your application and development environment configuration, more work may be required. Was this topic helpful?
